### 🐈‍⬛ Github Codes Link: [https://github.com/aryan-0077/CWA-LowLevelDesignCode](https://github.com/aryan-0077/CWA-LowLevelDesignCode)

## ‍

## 🧐Question :

You have the four functions:

• printFizz that prints the word "fizz" to the console,

• printBuzz that prints the word "buzz" to the console,

• printFizzBuzz that prints the word "fizzbuzz" to the console, and

• printNumber that prints a given integer to the console.

‍

You are given an instance of the class FizzBuzz that has four functions: fizz, buzz, fizzbuzz and number. The same instance of FizzBuzz will be passed to four different threads:

‍

• Thread A: calls fizz() that should output the word "fizz".

• Thread B: calls buzz() that should output the word "buzz".

• Thread C: calls fizzbuzz() that should output the word "fizzbuzz".

• Thread D: calls number() that should only output the integers.

‍

Modify the given class to output the series \[1, 2, "fizz", 4, "buzz", ...\] where the ith token (1-indexed) of the series is:

• "fizzbuzz" if i is divisible by 3 and 5,

• "fizz" if i is divisible by 3 and not 5,

• "buzz" if i is divisible by 5 and not 3, or

• if i is not divisible by 3 or 5.

‍

Implement the FizzBuzz class:

• FizzBuzz(int n) Initializes the object with the number n that represents the length of the sequence that should be printed.

• void fizz(printFizz) Calls printFizz to output "fizz".

• void buzz(printBuzz) Calls printBuzz to output "buzz".

• void fizzbuzz(printFizzBuzz) Calls printFizzBuzz to output "fizzbuzz".

• void number(printNumber) Calls printnumber to output the numbers.

‍

```
🚧 Constraints: 
▫️ 1 <= n <= 50
```

```
Example 1:
Input: n = 15
Output: 
[1,2,"fizz",4,"buzz","fizz",7,8,"fizz","buzz",11,"fizz",13,14,"fizzbuzz"]

Example 2:
Input: n = 5
Output: [1,2,"fizz",4,"buzz"]
```

‍

> 🔗 Problem link : [https://leetcode.com/problems/fizz-buzz-multithreaded/description/](https://leetcode.com/problems/fizz-buzz-multithreaded/description/%E2%80%8D)

[‍](https://leetcode.com/problems/fizz-buzz-multithreaded/description/%E2%80%8D)

```
Level : Medium
Topics : Concurrency 
Companies :  Gartner
```

‍‍

## 💡Solution :

### Understanding the Challenge : 

The main challenge is synchronizing the execution of four different threads so that they print numbers and strings in the correct sequence. We need to:

• Ensure only one thread prints at a time.

• Control execution order so that numbers, "fizz", "buzz", and "fizzbuzz" appear at the right moments.

• Avoid race conditions and deadlocks.

‍

### 🧩 Approach 1 : Using Semaphores for Thread Synchronization

_🧠_ Intuition :

```
We'll use four semaphores to control the flow:
	• numberSemaphore: Controls when numbers can be printed.
	• fizzSemaphore: Controls when "fizz" can be printed.
	• buzzSemaphore: Controls when "buzz" can be printed.
	• fizzBuzzSemaphore: Controls when "fizzbuzz" can be printed.
Each thread will wait for its respective semaphore before printing and then release the numberSemaphore for the next iteration.
```

‍

## 🤖 Algorithm :

1\. Initialize four semaphores:

○ numberSemaphore with 1 permit (starts first).

○ fizzSemaphore, buzzSemaphore, fizzBuzzSemaphore with 0 permits (blocked initially).

‍

2\. In number(), check conditions and release the appropriate semaphore.

‍

3.In fizz(), buzz(), and fizzbuzz(), wait for their semaphores, print, and then release numberSemaphore.

‍

## 📝 Implementation : 

Java

```
1class FizzBuzz { 
2    // Number up to which FizzBuzz needs to be calculated
3    private int n; 
4
5    // Semaphore to allow only the correct thread to print numbers
6    private Semaphore numberSemaphore; 
7
8    // Semaphore to allow the "Fizz" thread to print
9    private Semaphore fizzSemaphore; 
10
11    // Semaphore to allow the "Buzz" thread to print
12    private Semaphore buzzSemaphore; 
13
14    // Semaphore to allow the "FizzBuzz" thread to print
15    private Semaphore fizzBuzzSemaphore; 
16
17    // Constructor to initialize semaphores and set the limit
18    public FizzBuzz(int n) { 
19        this.n = n; 
20
21        // Initially allow printing of numbers
22        numberSemaphore = new Semaphore(1); 
23
24        // Block "Fizz", "Buzz", and "FizzBuzz" threads initially
25        fizzSemaphore = new Semaphore(0); 
26        buzzSemaphore = new Semaphore(0); 
27        fizzBuzzSemaphore = new Semaphore(0); 
28    } 
29
30    // Method to print "Fizz" if the number is divisible by 3 but not by 5
31    public void fizz(Runnable printFizz) throws InterruptedException { 
32        for(int i = 1; i <= n; i++) { 
33            if(i % 3 == 0 && i % 5 != 0) { 
34                // Wait for permission to print "Fizz"
35                fizzSemaphore.acquire(); 
36
37                // Execute the provided function to print "Fizz"
38                printFizz.run(); 
39
40               // Signal the next thread to proceed
41                numberSemaphore.release(); 
42            } 
43        } 
44    } 
45
46    // Method to print "Buzz" if the number is divisible by 5 but not by 3
47    public void buzz(Runnable printBuzz) throws InterruptedException { 
48        for(int i = 1; i <= n; i++) { 
49            if(i % 3 != 0 && i % 5 == 0) { 
50                // Wait for permission to print "Buzz"
51                buzzSemaphore.acquire(); 
52
53                // Execute the provided function to print "Buzz"
54                printBuzz.run(); 
55
56                // Signal the next thread to proceed
57                numberSemaphore.release(); 
58            } 
59        } 
60    } 
61
62    // Method to print "FizzBuzz" if the number is divisible by both 3 and 5
63    public void fizzbuzz(Runnable printFizzBuzz) throws InterruptedException { 
64        for(int i = 1; i <= n; i++) { 
65            if(i % 3 == 0 && i % 5 == 0) { 
66                // Wait for permission to print "FizzBuzz"
67                fizzBuzzSemaphore.acquire(); 
68
69                // Execute the provided function to print "FizzBuzz"
70                printFizzBuzz.run(); 
71
72                // Signal the next thread to proceed
73                numberSemaphore.release(); 
74            } 
75        } 
76    } 
77
78    // Method to print numbers if they are not divisible by 3 or 5
79    public void number(IntConsumer printNumber) throws InterruptedException { 
80        for(int i = 1; i <= n; i++) { 
81            // Wait for permission to print a number
82            numberSemaphore.acquire(); 
83
84            // Check divisibility and signal the appropriate thread
85            if(i % 3 == 0 && i % 5 == 0) { 
86                fizzBuzzSemaphore.release(); 
87            } else if(i % 3 == 0) { 
88                fizzSemaphore.release(); 
89            } else if(i % 5 == 0) { 
90                buzzSemaphore.release(); 
91            } else { 
92                // Execute the provided function to print the number
93                printNumber.accept(i); 
94                // Allow the next thread to proceed
95                numberSemaphore.release(); 
96            } 
97        } 
98    } 
99}
```

‍

## 🤹🏼Dry Run with Visualization

Let's perform a dry run for n = 5:

‍

### Step 1: Initialization

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1745651413475-image.png)

The FizzBuzz class is initialized with n = 5, and four semaphores are created:

• numberSemaphore starts with 1 permit (allows first operation)

• fizzSemaphore starts with 0 permits (blocked)

• buzzSemaphore starts with 0 permits (blocked)

fizzBuzzSemaphore starts with 0 permits (blocked)

‍

### Step 2:Thread Creation and Start : 

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1745651478148-image.png)

Four threads are created and started:

• Thread A calls fizz()

• Thread B calls buzz()

• Thread C calls fizzbuzz()

Thread D calls number()

‍

### Step 3:Processing i=1

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1745651516562-image.png)

Thread D (number()) acquires numberSemaphore and checks i=1:

• 1 is not divisible by 3 or 5, so printNumber(1) is called

numberSemaphore is released for the next iteration

‍

### Step 4 : Processing i=2 : 

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1745651559347-image.png)

Thread D (number()) acquires numberSemaphore again and checks i=2:

• 2 is not divisible by 3 or 5, so printNumber(2) is called

numberSemaphore is released for the next iteration

‍

### Step 5:Processing i=3

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1745651594975-image.png)

Thread D (number()) acquires numberSemaphore and checks i=3:

• 3 is divisible by 3, so fizzSemaphore is released

• Thread A (fizz()) is waiting for fizzSemaphore, now it can proceed

Thread A calls printFizz() and releases numberSemaphore

‍

### Step 6:Processing i=4

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1745651702478-image.png)

Thread D (number()) acquires numberSemaphore and checks i=4:

• 4 is not divisible by 3 or 5, so printNumber(4) is called

numberSemaphore is released for the next iteration

‍

### Step 7:Processing i=5

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1745651752674-image.png)

Thread D (number()) acquires numberSemaphore and checks i=5:

• 5 is divisible by 5, so buzzSemaphore is released

• Thread B (buzz()) is waiting for buzzSemaphore, now it can proceed

Thread B calls printBuzz() and releases numberSemaphore

‍

## ‍📈 Complexity Analysis :

```
>⏳ Time Complexity : O(n) 
as each number is processed once.

>🗃️ Space Complexity : O(n)
as only a few semaphores are used.
```

‍

## 🗝️Key Insights : 

• **Semaphore Usage:** Perfect for controlling execution order in multi-threading.

• **Thread Coordination:** Ensures numbers, "fizz", "buzz", and "fizzbuzz" appear in the correct sequence.

• **No Busy Waiting:** Efficient execution using synchronization primitives.

‍

This approach guarantees correct and synchronized execution of the FizzBuzz problem using four threads.\n\n---\n\n## Source: https://codewitharyan.com/tech-blogs/design-bounded-blocking-queue\n\n# Design Bounded Blocking Queue

### 🐈‍⬛ Github Codes Link: [https://github.com/aryan-0077/CWA-LowLevelDesignCode](https://github.com/aryan-0077/CWA-LowLevelDesignCode)

## ‍

## 🧐Question :

Implement a thread-safe bounded blocking queue that has the following methods:

• BoundedBlockingQueue(int capacity) The constructor initializes the queue with a maximum capacity.

• void enqueue(int element) Adds an element to the front of the queue. If the queue is full, the calling thread is blocked until the queue is no longer full.

• int dequeue() Returns the element at the rear of the queue and removes it. If the queue is empty, the calling thread is blocked until the queue is no longer empty.

• int size() Returns the number of elements currently in the queue.

Your implementation will be tested using multiple threads at the same time. Each thread will either be a producer thread that only makes calls to the enqueue method or a consumer thread that only makes calls to the dequeue method. The size method will be called after every test case.

‍

```
🚧 Constraints: 
▫️1 <= Number of Prdoucers <= 8
▫️1 <= Number of Consumers <= 8
▫️1 <= size <= 30
▫️0 <= element <= 20
▫️The number of calls to enqueue is greater than or equal to the number of calls to dequeue.
At most 40 calls will be made to enque, deque, and size.
```

```
Example 1:
Input:
1
1
["BoundedBlockingQueue","enqueue","dequeue","dequeue","enqueue","enqueue","enqueue","enqueue","dequeue"]
[[2],[1],[],[],[0],[2],[3],[4],[]]

Output:
[1,0,2,2]
Explanation:
Number of producer threads = 1
Number of consumer threads = 1
BoundedBlockingQueue queue = new BoundedBlockingQueue(2);   // initialize the queue with capacity = 2.
queue.enqueue(1);   // The producer thread enqueues 1 to the queue.
queue.dequeue();    // The consumer thread calls dequeue and returns 1 from the queue.
queue.dequeue();    // Since the queue is empty, the consumer thread is blocked.
queue.enqueue(0);   // The producer thread enqueues 0 to the queue. The consumer thread is unblocked and returns 0 from the queue.
queue.enqueue(2);   // The producer thread enqueues 2 to the queue.
queue.enqueue(3);   // The producer thread enqueues 3 to the queue.
queue.enqueue(4);   // The producer thread is blocked because the queue's capacity (2) is reached.
queue.dequeue();    // The consumer thread returns 2 from the queue. The producer thread is unblocked and enqueues 4 to the queue.
queue.size();       // 2 elements remaining in the queue. size() is always called at the end of each test case.


Example 2:
Input:
3
4
["BoundedBlockingQueue","enqueue","enqueue","enqueue","dequeue","dequeue","dequeue","enqueue"]
[[3],[1],[0],[2],[],[],[],[3]]

Output:
[1,0,2,1]

Explanation:
Number of producer threads = 3
Number of consumer threads = 4
BoundedBlockingQueue queue = new BoundedBlockingQueue(3);   // initialize the queue with capacity = 3.
queue.enqueue(1);   // Producer thread P1 enqueues 1 to the queue.
queue.enqueue(0);   // Producer thread P2 enqueues 0 to the queue.
queue.enqueue(2);   // Producer thread P3 enqueues 2 to the queue.
queue.dequeue();    // Consumer thread C1 calls dequeue.
queue.dequeue();    // Consumer thread C2 calls dequeue.
queue.dequeue();    // Consumer thread C3 calls dequeue.
queue.enqueue(3);   // One of the producer threads enqueues 3 to the queue.
queue.size();       // 1 element remaining in the queue.
Since the number of threads for producer/consumer is greater than 1, we do not know how the threads will be scheduled in the operating system, even though the input seems to imply the ordering. Therefore, any of the output [1,0,2] or [1,2,0] or [0,1,2] or [0,2,1] or [2,0,1] or [2,1,0] will be accepted.
```

‍

> 🔗 Problem link : [https://leetcode.com/problems/design-bounded-blocking-queue/description/](https://leetcode.com/problems/design-bounded-blocking-queue/description/)

‍

```
Level : Medium
Topics : Concurrency 
Companies :  Linkedin, Tesla, Rubrik
```

‍‍

## 💡Solution :

The problem requires a thread-safe implementation of a bounded blocking queue that supports concurrent access. We can use Semaphores to handle blocking behavior for enqueue and dequeue operations efficiently.

‍

### 🧩 Approach:

_🧠_ Intuition :

```
A bounded blocking queue is a queue that allows concurrent access but limits the number of elements that can be stored at a given time. The challenge lies in ensuring that:
	• Producers cannot insert elements when the queue is full and must wait.
	• Consumers cannot remove elements when the queue is empty and must wait.
	• The operations must be thread-safe to handle multiple producer and consumer threads simultaneously.
To achieve this, we use semaphores to control access to the queue, preventing race conditions and ensuring proper synchronization.
```

‍

1\. **Use a Semaphore :** 

• empty semaphore to track available slots.

• full semaphore to track the number of occupied slots.

‍

2\. **Use a Concurrent Data Structure :** 

• A ConcurrentLinkedDeque is used for safe concurrent access.

‍

3\. **Thread Synchronization :** 

• enqueue() acquires an empty slot before inserting.

• dequeue() acquires a full slot before removing an element.

‍

## 📝 Implementation : 

Java

```
1class BoundedBlockingQueue { 
2    // Semaphore to track the number of items in the queue (full spots)
3    private Semaphore full; 
4
5    // Semaphore to track the number of available empty spots in the queue
6    private Semaphore empty; 
7
8    // A concurrent deque to store the elements of the queue
9    private ConcurrentLinkedDeque<Integer> deque; 
10
11    // Constructor to initialize the semaphores and the deque
12    public BoundedBlockingQueue(int capacity) { 
13        // 'full' starts with 0 since the queue is empty initially
14        full = new Semaphore(0); 
15        // 'empty' starts with the capacity of the queue
16        empty = new Semaphore(capacity); 
17        // Initialize the concurrent deque to hold queue elements
18        deque = new ConcurrentLinkedDeque<>(); 
19    } 
20
21    // Method to add an element to the queue
22    public void enqueue(int element) throws InterruptedException { 
23        // Acquire an empty spot before adding an element
24        empty.acquire(); 
25        // Add the element to the front of the deque
26        deque.addFirst(element); 
27        // Release a full spot after adding the element
28        full.release(); 
29    } 
30
31    // Method to remove and retrieve an element from the queue
32    public int dequeue() throws InterruptedException { 
33        int result = -1; 
34        // Acquire a full spot before removing an element
35        full.acquire(); 
36        // Remove the element from the end of the deque
37        result = deque.pollLast(); 
38        // Release an empty spot after removing the element
39        empty.release(); 
40        return result; 
41    } 
42
43    // Method to get the current size of the queue
44    public int size() throws InterruptedException { 
45        int result = 0; 
46        // Retrieve and return the size of the deque
47        result = deque.size();
48        return result; 
49    } 
50}
```

‍

## 🤹🏼Dry Run with Visualization

Let's perform a dry run for Input : 

1

1

\["BoundedBlockingQueue","enqueue","dequeue","dequeue","enqueue","enqueue","enqueue","enqueue","dequeue"\]

\[\[2\],\[1\],\[\],\[\],\[0\],\[2\],\[3\],\[4\],\[\]\]

‍‍

### Step 1: Initilization

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1745859853555-Frame-237.png)

### ‍

### Step 2: First Enqueue Operation 

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1745859889172-Frame-292.png)

‍The producer thread calls enqueue(1):

• Acquires a permit from empty semaphore (now 1 permit left)

• Adds element 1 to the front of the queue

Releases a permit to full semaphore (now 1 permit)

‍

### Step 3: First Dequeue Operation : 

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1745859929206-Frame-238-\(1\).png)

The consumer thread calls dequeue():

• Acquires a permit from full semaphore (now 0 permits)

• Removes element 1 from the end of the queue

• Releases a permit to empty semaphore (now 2 permits)

Returns 1

‍‍

### Step 4 :  Second Dequeue Operation (Blocking) : 

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1745859955452-Frame-251.png)

The consumer thread calls dequeue() again, but the queue is empty:

• Tries to acquire a permit from full semaphore, but it's blocked (0 permits)

Thread waits until a permit becomes available

‍‍

### Step 5: Second Enqueue Operation (Unblocks Consumer) : 

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1745859978149-Frame-239.png)

The producer thread calls enqueue(0):

• Acquires a permit from empty semaphore (now 1 permit)

• Adds element 0 to the front of the queue

• Releases a permit to full semaphore (now 1 permit)

This unblocks the waiting consumer thread

‍‍

### Step 6: Third and Fourth Enqueue Operations : 

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1745860000340-Frame-241-\(6\).png)

The producer thread calls enqueue(2) and enqueue(3):

• Both operations succeed, filling the queue to capacity

After these operations, empty semaphore has 0 permits, and full semaphore has 2 permits

‍

### Step 7: Fifth Enqueue Operation (Blocking) : 

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1745860025189-Frame-242.png)

The producer thread calls enqueue(4), but the queue is full:

• Tries to acquire a permit from empty semaphore, but it's blocked (0 permits)

Thread waits until a permit becomes available

‍

### Step 8:Third Dequeue Operation (Unblocks Producer) : 

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1745860207974-Frame-241-\(7\).png)

The consumer thread calls dequeue():

• Acquires a permit from full semaphore (now 1 permit)

• Removes element 2 from the end of the queue (remember FIFO - first in, first out)

• Releases a permit to empty semaphore (now 1 permit)

• This unblocks the waiting producer thread

The producer thread then adds element 4 to the queue

‍

### Step 9: Final Size Check : 

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1745860224190-Frame-242-\(1\).png)

The size method is called to check the current size of the queue:

Returns 2 (elements 3 and 4 are in the queue)

‍

## ‍📈 Complexity Analysis :

```
>⏳ Time Complexity : 
enqueue(): O(1) (Adding to the front of the queue is constant time)
dequeue(): O(1) (Removing from the rear is constant time)

>🗃️ Space Complexity : 
size(): O(1) (ConcurrentLinkedDeque provides O(1) size retrieval)
```

‍

## 🗝️Key Insights : 

• Semaphores are used to control concurrent access and blocking behavior.

• ConcurrentLinkedDeque is used for thread-safe operations.

• Blocking behavior ensures producers wait if the queue is full and consumers wait if the queue is empty.

Efficient operations with O(1) complexity.\n\n---\n\n## Source: https://codewitharyan.com/tech-blogs/the-dining-philosophers\n\n# The Dining Philosophers

### 🐈‍⬛ Github Codes Link: [https://github.com/aryan-0077/CWA-LowLevelDesignCode](https://github.com/aryan-0077/CWA-LowLevelDesignCode)

## ‍

## 🧐Question :

Five silent philosophers sit at a round table with bowls of spaghetti. Forks are placed between each pair of adjacent philosophers.

‍

Each philosopher must alternately think and eat. However, a philosopher can only eat spaghetti when they have both left and right forks. Each fork can be held by only one philosopher and so a philosopher can use the fork only if it is not being used by another philosopher. After an individual philosopher finishes eating, they need to put down both forks so that the forks become available to others. A philosopher can take the fork on their right or the one on their left as they become available, but cannot start eating before getting both forks.

‍

Eating is not limited by the remaining amounts of spaghetti or stomach space; an infinite supply and an infinite demand are assumed.

‍

Design a discipline of behaviour (a concurrent algorithm) such that no philosopher will starve; i.e., each can forever continue to alternate between eating and thinking, assuming that no philosopher can know when others may want to eat or think.

‍

The philosophers' ids are numbered from 0 to 4 in a clockwise order. Implement the function void wantsToEat(philosopher, pickLeftFork, pickRightFork, eat, putLeftFork, putRightFork) where:

• philosopher is the id of the philosopher who wants to eat.

• pickLeftFork and pickRightFork are functions you can call to pick the corresponding forks of that philosopher.

• eat is a function you can call to let the philosopher eat once he has picked both forks.

• putLeftFork and putRightFork are functions you can call to put down the corresponding forks of that philosopher.

• The philosophers are assumed to be thinking as long as they are not asking to eat (the function is not being called with their number).

‍

Five threads, each representing a philosopher, will simultaneously use one object of your class to simulate the process. The function may be called for the same philosopher more than once, even before the last call ends.

‍![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1746232542830-Frame-237-\(7\).png)

```
🚧 Constraints: 
▫️	• 1 <= n <= 60
```

```
Example 1:
Input: n = 1

Output: [[4,2,1],[4,1,1],[0,1,1],[2,2,1],[2,1,1],[2,0,3],[2,1,2],[2,2,2],[4,0,3],[4,1,2],[0,2,1],[4,2,2],[3,2,1],[3,1,1],[0,0,3],[0,1,2],[0,2,2],[1,2,1],[1,1,1],[3,0,3],[3,1,2],[3,2,2],[1,0,3],[1,1,2],[1,2,2]]

Explanation:
n is the number of times each philosopher will call the function.
The output array describes the calls you made to the functions controlling the forks and the eat function, its format is:
output[i] = [a, b, c] (three integers)
- a is the id of a philosopher.
- b specifies the fork: {1 : left, 2 : right}.
- c specifies the operation: {1 : pick, 2 : put, 3 : eat}.
```

‍

> 🔗 Problem link :  [https://leetcode.com/problems/the-dining-philosophers/description/](https://leetcode.com/problems/the-dining-philosophers/description/)

‍

```
Level : Medium
Topics : Concurrency 
Companies :  Apple
```

‍‍

## 💡Solution :

### Understanding the Challenge :

The Dining Philosophers problem is a classic synchronization problem in computer science that illustrates challenges in resource allocation and deadlock prevention. We need to:

• Prevent deadlock (all philosophers holding one fork and waiting forever for another)

• Ensure no philosopher starves (each philosopher gets to eat eventually)

• Handle concurrent access to shared resources (forks)

• Implement proper synchronization between multiple threads

‍

### 🧩 Approach: Using Semaphores for Resource Management and Deadlock Prevention : 

_🧠_ Intuition :

```
We'll use two types of semaphores to manage the dining process:

• A counting semaphore to limit the number of philosophers who can attempt to eat simultaneously
• An array of binary semaphores to control access to individual forks

By allowing at most 4 philosophers to compete for resources simultaneously, we guarantee that at least one philosopher can acquire both forks and eat, preventing deadlock.
```

‍

## 🤖 Algorithm :

1\. Create a counting semaphore initialized to 4 permits to limit concurrent eating attempts

‍

2\. Create an array of 5 binary semaphores (one for each fork)

‍

3\. When a philosopher wants to eat: 

• Acquire the counting semaphore (limiting concurrent attempts)

• Determine the left and right fork indices

• Acquire semaphores for both forks

• Pick up forks, eat, then put down forks

• Release both fork semaphores

‍

Release the counting semaphore

‍

## 📝 Implementation : 

Java

```
1class DiningPhilosophers {
2  // Semaphore to limit the number of philosophers trying to eat simultaneously
3  private Semaphore semaphore;
4
5
6  // Array of semaphores to control access to each fork
7  private Semaphore[] forkSemaphore;
8
9
10  public DiningPhilosophers() {
11    // Allow at most 4 philosophers to try to eat at the same time
12    semaphore = new Semaphore(4);
13
14
15    // Initialize semaphores for each fork
16    forkSemaphore = new Semaphore[5];
17    for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++) forkSemaphore[i] = new Semaphore(1);
18  }
19
20
21  // call the run() method of any runnable to execute its code
22  public void wantsToEat(int philosopher, Runnable pickLeftFork, Runnable pickRightFork,
23      Runnable eat, Runnable putLeftFork, Runnable putRightFork) throws InterruptedException {
24    // Limit the number of philosophers who can try to eat simultaneously
25    semaphore.acquire();
26
27
28    // Identify the forks needed by this philosopher
29    int left = philosopher;
30    int right = (philosopher + 1) % 5;
31
32
33    // Get semaphores for the left and right forks
34    Semaphore leftForkSemaphore = forkSemaphore[left];
35    Semaphore rightForkSemaphore = forkSemaphore[right];
36
37
38    // Acquire both forks (resources)
39    leftForkSemaphore.acquire();
40    rightForkSemaphore.acquire();
41
42
43    // Perform the eating process
44    pickLeftFork.run();
45    pickRightFork.run();
46    eat.run();
47
48
49    // Put down the forks
50    putLeftFork.run();
51    leftForkSemaphore.release();
52    putRightFork.run();
53    rightForkSemaphore.release();
54
55
56    // Allow another philosopher to try to eat
57    semaphore.release();
58  }
59}
```

‍

## 🤹🏼Dry Run with Visualization

Let's perform a dry run with 5 philosophers (ids 0-4) at a round table:

### Step 1: Initialization :

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1746232776102-Frame-237-\(8\).png)

• A counting semaphore is created with 4 permits

• 5 binary semaphores are created for the 5 forks (one per fork)

• All semaphores are initially available

‍

### Step 2: Philosophers Try to Eat : 

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1746232671824-image.png)

Suppose all 5 philosophers get hungry simultaneously:

• Philosophers 0, 1, 2, and 3 acquire the counting semaphore (4 permits)

• Philosopher 4 is blocked waiting for the counting semaphore

‍

### Step 3: Resource Acquisition : 

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1746232692684-image.png)

Philosopher 0: 

• Acquires fork 0 (left) and fork 1 (right)

• Picks up both forks and eats

Philosopher 1: 

• Tries to acquire fork 1 (left) but it's held by Philosopher 0

• Waits for fork 1 to be released

‍

Philosopher 2: 

• Acquires fork 2 (left) and fork 3 (right)

• Picks up both forks and eats

‍

Philosopher 3: 

• Tries to acquire fork 3 (left) but it's held by Philosopher 2

• Waits for fork 3 to be released

‍

### Step 4: Resource Release : 

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1746232699804-image.png)

Philosopher 0 finishes eating: 

• Puts down both forks

• Releases fork semaphores 0 and 1

• Releases the counting semaphore

‍

Philosopher 4 acquires the counting semaphore

‍

Philosopher 1 acquires fork 1 (left) and fork 2 (right) 

• Picks up both forks and eats

‍

### Step 5: Continuous Operation : 

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1746232706600-image.png)

The process continues with philosophers acquiring and releasing resources, with the counting semaphore preventing deadlock by ensuring at least one philosopher can always acquire both forks.

‍

## ‍📈 Complexity Analysis :

```
>⏳ Time Complexity : 
O(1) for each call to wantsToEat

>🗃️ Space Complexity : 
 O(n) where n is the number of philosophers (5 in this case)‍
```

## ‍

## 🗝️Key Insights : 

1\. Deadlock Prevention:

By limiting the number of philosophers who can try to eat simultaneously to 4 (when there are 5 philosophers), we ensure that at least one philosopher can acquire both forks. This is because even in the worst case, 4 philosophers can only hold 4 forks, leaving 1 fork free, which means at least one philosopher can acquire both needed forks.

‍

2\. Resource Allocation Strategy:

Our implementation uses a straightforward approach where each philosopher attempts to acquire both forks at once. This prevents the classic deadlock scenario where each philosopher holds one fork and is waiting for another.

‍

3\. Fairness Considerations:

The semaphore implementation in Java does not guarantee fairness by default. For a more fair solution, we could use new Semaphore(4, true) which creates a fair semaphore that grants access in FIFO order.\n\n---\n\n## Source: https://codewitharyan.com/tech-blogs/web-crawler-multithreaded\n\n# Web Crawler Multithreaded

### 🐈‍⬛ Github Codes Link: [https://github.com/aryan-0077/CWA-LowLevelDesignCode](https://github.com/aryan-0077/CWA-LowLevelDesignCode)

## ‍

## 🧐Question :

Given a URL startUrl and an interface HtmlParser, implement a Multi-threaded web crawler to crawl all links that are under the same hostname as startUrl.

Return all URLs obtained by your web crawler in any order.

Your crawler should:

• Start from the page: startUrl

• Call HtmlParser.getUrls(url) to get all URLs from a webpage of a given URL.

• Do not crawl the same link twice.

Explore only the links that are under the same hostname as startUrl.

‍![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1746231768692-image.png)

As shown in the example URL above, the hostname is example.org. For simplicity's sake, you may assume all URLs use HTTP protocol without any port specified.

‍

For example, the URLs [http://leetcode.com/problems](http://leetcode.com/problems) and [http://leetcode.com/contest](http://leetcode.com/contest) are under the same hostname, while URLs [http://example.org/tes](http://example.org/tes)t and [http://example.com/abc](http://example.com/abc) are not under the same hostname.

‍

The HtmlParser interface is defined as such:

```
interface HtmlParser {
  // Return a list of all urls from a webpage of given url.
  // This is a blocking call, that means it will do HTTP request and return when this request is finished.
  public List<String> getUrls(String url);
}
```

Note that getUrls(String url) simulates performing an HTTP request. You can treat it as a blocking function call that waits for an HTTP request to finish. It is guaranteed that getUrls(String url) will return the URLs within 15ms. Single-threaded solutions will exceed the time limit so, can your multi-threaded web crawler do better?

‍

Below are two examples explaining the functionality of the problem. For custom testing purposes, you'll have three variables urls, edges and startUrl. Notice that you will only have access to startUrl in your code, while urls and edges are not directly accessible to you in code.

```
🚧 Constraints: 
▫️ 1 <= urls.length <= 1000
▫️ 1 <= urls[i].length <= 300
▫️startUrl is one of the urls.
▫️Hostname label must be from 1 to 63 characters long, including the dots, may contain only the ASCII letters from 'a' to 'z', digits from '0' to '9' and the hyphen-minus character ('-').
▫️The hostname may not start or end with the hyphen-minus character ('-'). 
You may assume there're no duplicates in the URL library.
```

‍‍

## Examples : 

Example 1:

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1746231704165-Frame-237-\(6\).png)

```
Input:
urls = [
 "http://news.yahoo.com",
 "http://news.yahoo.com/news",
 "http://news.yahoo.com/news/topics/",
 "http://news.google.com",
 "http://news.yahoo.com/us"
]
	
edges = [[2,0],[2,1],[3,2],[3,1],[0,4]]
startUrl = "http://news.yahoo.com/news/topics/"

Output: [
 "http://news.yahoo.com",
 "http://news.yahoo.com/news",
 "http://news.yahoo.com/news/topics/",
 "http://news.yahoo.com/us"
]
```

‍

Example 2 : 

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1746231524116-Frame-237-\(5\).png)

```
Input: 
urls = [
 "http://news.yahoo.com",
 "http://news.yahoo.com/news",
 "http://news.yahoo.com/news/topics/",
 "http://news.google.com"
]

edges = [[0,2],[2,1],[3,2],[3,1],[3,0]]
startUrl = "http://news.google.com"

Output: [http://news.google.com]
```

‍

> 🔗 Problem link :  [https://leetcode.com/problems/web-crawler-multithreaded/description/](https://leetcode.com/problems/web-crawler-multithreaded/description/)

‍

```
Level : Medium
Topics : Concurrency , BFS , DFS
Companies :  Anthropic , Databricks  Rubrik ,OpenAI , Meta , Microsoft, MongoDB, Dropbox
```

‍‍

## 💡Solution :

### Understanding the Challenge :

The problem requires us to create a multi-threaded web crawler that explores all URLs under the same hostname as a given starting URL. Key challenges include:

• Ensuring URLs aren't crawled multiple times

• Limiting crawling to URLs with the same hostname

• Implementing an efficient multi-threaded solution to meet time constraints

Coordinating multiple threads and managing thread synchronization

‍

### 🧩 Approach: Using Thread Pool and Concurrent Data Structures : 

_🧠_ Intuition :

> We'll use a thread pool to parallelize the crawling process, with each URL being processed by a worker thread. To avoid duplicates and handle concurrency, we'll use thread-safe data structures and atomic operations.

‍‍

## Algorithm:

1\. Extract the hostname from the startUrl

‍

2\. Initialize a thread pool with a fixed number of worker threads

‍

3\. Use a ConcurrentHashMap to track visited URLs

‍

4\. Create a Task class that: 

• Parses a URL

• Extracts links from the page

• Filters links by hostname

• Submits new tasks for unvisited URLs

‍

5\. Use an AtomicInteger to track the number of URLs being processed

‍

6\. Wait until all URLs have been processed before terminating

Return the set of discovered URLs

‍

## 📝 Implementation : 

Java

```
1class Solution {
2  // Store the hostname from startUrl to filter URLs
3  private String hostName;
4
5  // Thread-safe map to store visited URLs and avoid duplicates
6  private ConcurrentHashMap<String, Boolean> urlHashMap = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
7
8  // Thread pool to process URLs concurrently
9  private ExecutorService executor = Executors.newFixedThreadPool(5);
10
11  // Counter to track URLs to be processed
12  private AtomicInteger numOfUrlsToParse = new AtomicInteger(0);
13
14  // Reference to the HtmlParser
15  private HtmlParser htmlParser;
16
17  // Worker Task that processes URLs in separate threads
18  class Task implements Runnable {
19    private String url;
20
21    Task(String url) {
22      this.url = url;
23    }
24
25    public void run() {
26      // Get all URLs from the current page
27      for (String extractedUrl : htmlParser.getUrls(url)) {
28        // Extract hostname from URL
29        String curHostName = extractedUrl.split("/")[2];
30        // Check if URL has same hostname and hasn't been visited
31        if (curHostName.equals(hostName) && urlHashMap.putIfAbsent(extractedUrl, true) == null) {
32          // Increment counter for active tasks
33          numOfUrlsToParse.addAndGet(1);
34          // Submit new task to process this URL
35          executor.submit(new Task(extractedUrl));
36        }
37      }
38      // Decrement counter when task is complete
39      numOfUrlsToParse.addAndGet(-1);
40    }
41  }
42
43  // startUrl = "http://news.yahoo.com/news/topics/"
44  public List crawl(String startUrl, HtmlParser htmlParser) {
45    // Extract hostname from startUrl
46    // startUrl Split Array = ["http:", "", "news.yahoo.com", "news", "topics", ""]
47    // hostname = "news.yahoo.com"
48    hostName = startUrl.split("/")[2];
49    this.htmlParser = htmlParser;
50
51    // Mark startUrl as visited
52    urlHashMap.put(startUrl, true);
53
54    // Initialize counter and submit first task
55    numOfUrlsToParse.addAndGet(1);
56    executor.submit(new Task(startUrl));
57
58    // Wait until all URLs have been processed
59    while (numOfUrlsToParse.get() > 0) {
60      try {
61        // Sleep to avoid busy waiting
62        Thread.sleep(80);
63      } catch (Exception e) {
64        // Handle exceptions
65      }
66    }
67
68    // Shutdown the thread pool
69    executor.shutdown();
70
71    // Return all discovered URLs
72    return new ArrayList<>(urlHashMap.keySet());
73  }
74}
```

‍

## 🤹🏼Dry Run with Visualization

Let's perform a dry run using Example 1:

### Step 1: Initialization : 

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1746230865631-Frame-237-\(1\).png)

• startUrl = "http://news.yahoo.com/news/topics/"

• hostName = "news.yahoo.com"

• Create thread pool with 5 threads

• Add startUrl to urlHashMap

• Set numOfUrlsToParse to 1

• Submit Task for startUrl

‍

### Step 2: First URL Processing : 

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1746230924249-Frame-237-\(2\).png)

• Task processes "http://news.yahoo.com/news/topics/"

• HtmlParser.getUrls returns \["http://news.yahoo.com", "http://news.yahoo.com/news"\]

• Both URLs match hostName "news.yahoo.com"

• Add both to urlHashMap

• Increment numOfUrlsToParse & it becomes 3

• Submit two new Tasks

‍

### Step 3: Parallel URL Processing : 

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1746231075236-Frame-237-\(3\).png)

• Tasks for "http://news.yahoo.com" and "http://news.yahoo.com/news" run in parallel

• Task for "http://news.yahoo.com" discovers "http://news.yahoo.com/us"

• Task for "http://news.yahoo.com/news" doesn't find new URLs

• Add "http://news.yahoo.com/us" to urlHashMap

‍

### Step 4: Final URL Processing : 

![Article image](https://cwa-prod.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/1746231118544-Frame-237-\(4\).png)

• Task for "http://news.yahoo.com/us" runs

• No new URLs are discovered

• Decrement numOfUrlsToParse to 0

Main thread detects numOfUrlsToParse = 0 and returns results

‍

## ‍📈 Complexity Analysis :

```
>⏳ Time Complexity : 
O(V + E) where V is the number of URLs (vertices) and E is the number of links (edges)
The multi-threaded approach significantly reduces actual execution time by parallelizing requestsenqueue(): O(1) (Adding to the front of the queue is constant time)
dequeue(): O(1) (Removing from the rear is constant time)

>🗃️ Space Complexity : 
O(V) where V is the number of URLs
Space is primarily used by the ConcurrentHashMap to store discovered URLs
```

‍

## 🗝️Key Insights : 

**Concurrency Control:**

The solution uses three key Java concurrency components:

1\. ExecutorService: Manages the thread pool and task submission

2\. ConcurrentHashMap: Provides thread-safe access to the visited URLs collection

3\. AtomicInteger: Safely tracks the number of active tasks across threads

‍

**Duplicate Prevention:**

• The putIfAbsent method of ConcurrentHashMap atomically adds a URL if it doesn't exist and returns null if added successfully

• This ensures each URL is processed exactly once even with multiple concurrent threads

‍

**Termination Condition:**

• The AtomicInteger counter tracks how many tasks are currently being processed.

• The main thread waits until this counter reaches zero, indicating all URLs have been processed.

‍

**Performance Considerations:** 

• Using a fixed-sized thread pool of 5 threads balances parallelism with overhead

• The sleep(80) in the waiting loop avoids busy waiting and reduces CPU usage

• The 80ms sleep duration is chosen as it's longer than the guaranteed 15ms for getUrls() to complete\n\n---\n\n## Source: https://codewitharyan.com/tech-blogs/design-movie-ticket-booking-system\n\n# Design Movie Ticket Booking System

Topic Tags:

System DesignLLD

### 🐈‍⬛ Github Codes Link: [https://github.com/aryan-0077/CWA-LowLevelDesignCode](https://github.com/aryan-0077/CWA-LowLevelDesignCode)

# ‍

# Low-Level Design: Movie Ticket Booking System 🎬

A movie ticket booking system is designed to efficiently manage movie shows, seat reservations, and payment processing in a theatre. The system needs to handle multiple concurrent requests, manage seat availability states, and ensure a smooth user experience while maintaining data consistency.

‍

**Setup:**

• The theatre has multiple screens, each with a specific seating arrangement.

• Each screen can host multiple shows for different movies throughout the day.

• Users can browse available shows and select seats for booking.

• Each booking session has a configurable timeout period.

‍

**Operation:**

• Users can view all available shows and select a particular show.

• Users can view available seats for a selected show.

• Users can select seats, which become temporarily unavailable to other users.

• Users must complete payment within the session timeout period.

• The system handles concurrent booking requests and prevents double bookings.

‍

**Safety Features:**

• Seat locking mechanism to prevent double booking.

• Session timeout to release locked seats if payment isn't completed.

• Notification system for users when selected seats become unavailable.

‍

## Interview Setting 🤝 : 

### Point 1: Introduction and Vague Problem Statement:

Interviewer: Let's start with a basic problem statement. Design a Movie Ticket Booking System.

‍

Candidate: Certainly! Here's my understanding of the Movie Ticket Booking System:

• The system will manage multiple shows for different movies across screens in a theatre.

• Users can browse shows and select seats for booking.

• The system needs to handle concurrent seat selections and prevent double bookings.

• A booking process includes seat selection, payment, and ticket generation.

• The system should be scalable and maintain data consistency.

Is this the expected direction?

‍

Interviewer: Yes, you are aligned with the direction. Please continue ahead.

‍

Candidate: Great! Before diving into the design, I'd like to clarify a few requirements:

• How should we handle concurrent users trying to book the same seats?

• What happens if a user selects seats but doesn't complete payment?

• Are there any timing constraints for completing a booking?

‍

### Point 2: Clarifying Requirements:

Interviewer: We want a system that:

• Supports multiple screens in a theatre showing different movies.

• Handles both browse requests (viewing shows/seats) and booking requests.

• Efficiently manages concurrent booking attempts to prevent double bookings.

• Implements session timeouts to release seats if payment isn't completed.

‍

Candidate: To summarize, the key requirements are:

• A theatre with multiple screens and shows.

• User authentication and booking session management.

• Temporary seat locking during the booking process.

• Seat status tracking (available, temporarily unavailable, permanently unavailable).

• Session timeout to handle abandoned bookings.

‍

Interviewer: Perfect, let's proceed.

‍

### Point 3: Identifying Key Components:

Candidate: Now that we have the requirements, let's identify the key components of our Movie Ticket Booking system:

1\. Theatre: Represents the physical theatre with screens. 

2\. Screen: Represents a movie screen within the theatre. 

3\. Movie: Represents a movie that can be shown. 

4\. Show: Represents a movie screening at a specific time and screen. 

5\. Seat: Represents an individual seat in a screen.

6\. User: Represents a registered user of the system. 

7\. SeatLockProvider : Common Interface for Locking Mechanisms during Ticket Booking

‍

State Management with Enums: To effectively manage seat statuses, we'll use enums:

‍

Java

```
1// Enum to represent the status of a booking session
2
3public enum BookingStatus {
4  CREATED,// Booking has been created but not yet confirmed
5  CONFIRMED, // Booking has been successfully confirmed
6  EXPIRED; // Booking has expired due to timeout or other factors
7}
8
9// Enum to represent seat categories in a movie ticket booking system.
10public enum SeatCategory {
11  SILVER, // Standard seating with basic amenities
12  GOLD, // Premium seating with better view and comfort
13  PLATINUM; // Luxurious seating with highest comfort and exclusive benefits
14}
```

‍

Interviewer: That sounds good. Let's proceed with the design details.

‍

### Point 4: Design Challenges:

Interviewer: What design challenges do you anticipate?

‍

Candidate: The key challenges for the Movie Ticket Booking system include:

• Concurrency Management: Handling multiple users trying to book the same seats simultaneously.

• Session Management: Tracking and timing out booking sessions to prevent seat hoarding.

• Payment Integration: Managing the payment process with retry capabilities.

• Scalability: Supporting multiple theatres, screens, and concurrent users.

‍

### Point 5: Approach:

Interviewer: How would you approach these challenges?

‍

Candidate: I propose using design patterns effectively. Here are my strategies:

1\. **Repository Pattern:** 

• We have different Services like MovieService and TheatreService acting as repositories, managing collections of entities.

• They handle creation, retrieval, and management of domain objects.

‍

2\. **Strategy Pattern for Seat Locking Mechanism :** 

• Defines a family of Locking algorithms and makes them interchangeable. 

• Allows the booking system to support various locking methods (e.g., database-backed or distributed lock providers) to be used without changing the dependent code.

‍

3.  **Controller/Handler/Manager -> Service Pattern for Clean Architecture :** 

• Promotes separation of concerns by delegating HTTP request handling to controllers and business logic to service classes.

• Enables a clean, maintainable, and scalable architecture that aligns well with popular frameworks like Spring Boot, Django, and Express.js.

‍

4\. **Dependency Injection:** 

• The system uses constructor-based dependency injection to provide required services.

• For example, the BookingService requires a SeatLockProvider which is injected through its constructor.

‍

5\. **Factory Method Pattern for Entity Creation:** 

• Centralizes the creation logic for complex objects like Movies, Shows, and Theatres

• Encapsulates instantiation logic in dedicated factory methods, making it easier to change creation algorithms.

‍

6.  **Observer Pattern for Notification System:** 

• Allows for adding multiple notification channels (email, SMS, push notifications) without modifying the booking logic

• Provides real-time updates to users about their bookings and relevant show information

‍

Interviewer: That sounds like a solid approach. Let's delve into the implementation details.

‍

### Point 6: Implementation:

Interviewer: Ready to discuss implementation?

‍

Candidate: Yes. I'll focus on a modular and scalable design that meets the core Movie Ticket Booking system requirements.

‍

```
1public class Booking {
2    private final String id; // Unique identifier for this booking.
3    private final Show show; // The specific show for which the booking is made.
4    private final List<Seat> seatsBooked; // The list of seats that are part of this booking.
5    private final User user; // The user who made this booking.
6    private BookingStatus bookingStatus; // The current status of the booking (e.g., Created, Confirmed, Expired).
7
8    public Booking(final String id, final Show show, final User user, final List<Seat> seatsBooked) {
9        this.id = id;
10        this.show = show;
11        this.seatsBooked = seatsBooked;
12        this.user = user;
13        this.bookingStatus = BookingStatus.CREATED; // Initial booking status is set to Created.
14    }
15
16    public boolean isConfirmed() {
17        return this.bookingStatus == BookingStatus.CONFIRMED;
18    }
19
20    public void confirmBooking() throws Exception {
21        if (this.bookingStatus != BookingStatus.CREATED) {
22            throw new Exception("Cannot confirm a booking that is not in the Created state.");
23        }
24        this.bookingStatus = BookingStatus.CONFIRMED; // Update the booking status to Confirmed.
25    }
26
27    public void expireBooking() throws Exception {
28        if (this.bookingStatus != BookingStatus.CREATED) {
29            throw new Exception("Cannot expire a booking that is not in the Created state.");
30        }
31        this.bookingStatus = BookingStatus.EXPIRED; // Update the booking status to expire.
32    }
33
34    // Getters Section Start
35    public String getId() {
36        return id;
37    }
38
39    public Show getShow() {
40        return show;
41    }
42
43    public List<Seat> getSeatsBooked() {
44        return seatsBooked;
45    }
46
47    public User getUser() {
48        return user;
49    }
50
51    public BookingStatus getBookingStatus() {
52        return bookingStatus;
53    }
54    // Getters Section End
55}
```

‍

• **Seat Lock Class (V.V. Important):** 

This class is designed to represent a temporary lock on a specific seat. These locks are acquired by users during the booking process to prevent concurrent bookings of the same seat by multiple users. The purpose of this class is to manage and track which seats are temporarily held, ensuring that only one user can ultimately book a given seat at a time

‍

Java

```
1public class SeatLock {
2    private Seat seat; // The specific seat that is locked.
3    private Show show; // The show for which the seat is locked.
4    private Integer timeoutInSeconds; // The duration for which the lock is valid, in seconds.
5    private Date lockTime; // The timestamp when the lock was acquired.
6    private User lockedBy; // Identifier of the user or process that holds the lock.
7
8    public SeatLock(Seat seat, Show show, Integer timeoutInSeconds, Date date, User user) {
9        this.seat = seat;
10        this.show = show;
11        this.timeoutInSeconds = timeoutInSeconds;
12        this.lockTime = date;
13        this.lockedBy = user;
14    }
15
16    public boolean isLockExpired() {
17        final Instant lockInstant = lockTime.toInstant().plusSeconds(timeoutInSeconds);
18        final Instant currentInstant = new Date().toInstant();
19        return lockInstant.isBefore(currentInstant);
20    }
21
22    public Seat getSeat() {
23        return seat;
24    }
25    public void setSeat(Seat seat) {
26        this.seat = seat;
27    }
28
29    public Show getShow() {
30        return show;
31    }
32    public void setShow(Show show) {
33        this.show = show;
34    }
35    public Integer getTimeoutInSeconds() {
36        return timeoutInSeconds;
37    }
38
39    public void setTimeoutInSeconds(Integer timeoutInSeconds) {
40        this.timeoutInSeconds = timeoutInSeconds;
41    }
42
43    public Date getLockTime() {
44        return lockTime;
45    }
46
47    public void setLockTime(Date lockTime) {
48        this.lockTime = lockTime;
49    }
50
51    public User getLockedBy() {
52        return lockedBy;
53    }
54
55    public void setLockedBy(User lockedBy) {
56        this.lockedBy = lockedBy;
57    }
58}
```

‍

### 2\. Implementing ISeatLockProvider for Dependency Inversion : 

• **ISeatLockProvider Interface :** 

This interface defines the contract for any class that provides seat locking functionality in the movie ticket booking system. It outlines the essential operations for temporarily reserving seats for users during the booking process, ensuring that different locking mechanisms can be used interchangeably within the BookingService.

‍

Java

```
1public interface ISeatLockProvider {
2	    void lockSeats(Show show, List<Seat> seat, User user) throws Exception;
3	    void unlockSeats(Show show, List<Seat> seat, User user);
4	    boolean validateLock(Show show, Seat seat, User user);
5	    List<Seat> getLockedSeats(Show show);
6	}
```

‍

**• Concrete Lock Providers :** 

**• Seat Lock Provider :** 

This is a concrete implementation of the SeatLockProvider interface that manages seat locks in the application's memory. It uses a Map to store the lock information, associating shows and seats with the user who locked them and the lock's expiration time. This provider is suitable for scenarios where persistence of locks across application restarts is not required.

‍

Java

```
1public class SeatLockProvider implements ISeatLockProvider {
2  // Timeout in seconds after which a lock expires
3  private final Integer lockTimeout;
4
5  // Stores the locks per show and seat
6  private final Map<Show, Map<Seat, SeatLock>> locks;
7
8  // Constructor to initialize lock store and timeout
9  public SeatLockProvider(Integer lockTimeout) {
10    this.locks = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
11    this.lockTimeout = lockTimeout;
12  }
13
14  @Override
15  public void lockSeats(final Show show, final List<Seat> seats,
16      final User user) throws Exception {
17    // Get or initialize the seat lock map for this show
18    Map<Seat, SeatLock> seatLocks =
19        locks.computeIfAbsent(show, s -> new ConcurrentHashMap<>());
20    synchronized (seatLocks) {
21      // Check if any of the requested seats is already locked and the lock is
22      // still valid
23      for (Seat seat : seats) {
24        if (seatLocks.containsKey(seat)) {
25          SeatLock existingLock = seatLocks.get(seat);
26          if (!existingLock.isLockExpired()) {
27            throw new Exception(
28                "Seat " + seat.getSeatId() + " is already locked.");
29          }
30        }
31      }
32      // All seats available; lock them together
33      Date now = new Date();
34      for (Seat seat : seats) {
35        SeatLock lock = new SeatLock(seat, show, lockTimeout, now, user);
36        seatLocks.put(seat, lock);
37      }
38    }
39  }
40
41  @Override
42  public void unlockSeats(
43      final Show show, final List<Seat> seats, final User user) {
44    Map<Seat, SeatLock> seatLocks = locks.get(show);
45    if (seatLocks == null)
46      return;
47    synchronized (seatLocks) {
48      for (Seat seat : seats) {
49        SeatLock lock = seatLocks.get(seat);
50        if (lock != null && lock.getLockedBy().equals(user)) {
51          seatLocks.remove(seat);
52        }
53      }
54    }
55  }
56
57  @Override
58  public boolean validateLock(
59      final Show show, final Seat seat, final User user) {
60    Map<Seat, SeatLock> seatLocks = locks.get(show);
61    if (seatLocks == null)
62      return false;
63    synchronized (seatLocks) {
64      SeatLock lock = seatLocks.get(seat);
65      return lock != null && !lock.isLockExpired()
66          && lock.getLockedBy().equals(user);
67    }
68  }
69
70  @Override
71  public List<Seat> getLockedSeats(final Show show) {
72    Map<Seat, SeatLock> seatLocks = locks.get(show);
73    if (seatLocks == null) {
74      return Collections.emptyList();
75    }
76    synchronized (seatLocks) {
77      return seatLocks.entrySet()
78          .stream()
79          .filter(entry -> !entry.getValue().isLockExpired())
80          .map(Map.Entry::getKey)
81          .collect(Collectors.toList());
82    }
83  }
84}
```

‍

> Note : Other Lock Providers can be Third Party Lock Providers or another algorithmic lock providers

‍

### 3\. Controller -> Service Pattern for Clean Architecture : 

• **Implementing Core Services :** 

‍

_Movie Service :_ 

Java

```
1public class MovieService {
2	    private final Map<Integer, Movie> movies;
3	    private final AtomicInteger movieCounter; // Private counter for generating movie IDs
4	    public MovieService() {
5	        this.movies = new HashMap<>();
6	        this.movieCounter = new AtomicInteger(0); // Initialize the counter to 0
7	    }
8
9
10	    public Movie getMovie(final int movieId) throws Exception {
11	        if (!movies.containsKey(movieId)) {
12	            throw new Exception("Movie with ID " + movieId + " not found.");
13	        }
14	        return movies.get(movieId);
15	    }
16	    public Movie createMovie(final String movieName, final int durationInMinutes) {
17	        int movieId = movieCounter.incrementAndGet(); // Increment the counter and get the new value.
18	        Movie movie = new Movie(movieId, movieName, durationInMinutes);
19	        movies.put(movieId, movie);
20	        return movie;
21	    }
22	}
```

‍

_Theatre Service_

Java

```
1public class TheatreService {
2
3	    // Maps to hold all created theatres, screens, and seats
4	    private final Map<Integer, Theatre> theatres;
5	    private final Map<Integer, Screen> screens;
6	    private final Map<Integer, Seat> seats;
7
8	    // Atomic counters for generating unique IDs
9	    private final AtomicInteger theatreCounter;
10	    private final AtomicInteger screenCounter;
11	    private final AtomicInteger seatCounter;
12	    // Constructor initializing all maps and counters
13	    public TheatreService() {
14	        this.theatres = new HashMap<>();
15	        this.screens = new HashMap<>();
16	        this.seats = new HashMap<>();
17	        this.theatreCounter = new AtomicInteger(0);
18	        this.screenCounter = new AtomicInteger(0);
19	        this.seatCounter = new AtomicInteger(0);
20	    }
21	    // Retrieves a seat by ID, throws exception if not found
22	    public Seat getSeat(final int seatId) throws Exception {
23	        if (!seats.containsKey(seatId)) {
24	            throw new Exception("Seat with ID " + seatId + " not found.");
25	        }
26	        return seats.get(seatId);
27	    }
28
29	    // Retrieves a theatre by ID, throws exception if not found
30	    public Theatre getTheatre(final int theatreId) throws Exception{
31	        if (!theatres.containsKey(theatreId)) {
32	            throw new Exception("Theatre with ID " + theatreId + " not found.");
33	        }
34	        return theatres.get(theatreId);
35	    }
36	    // Retrieves a screen by ID, throws exception if not found
37	    public Screen getScreen(final int screenId) throws Exception  {
38	        if (!screens.containsKey(screenId)) {
39	            throw new Exception("Screen with ID " + screenId + " not found.");
40	        }
41	        return screens.get(screenId);
42	    }
43	    // Creates a new theatre with a unique ID and stores it
44	    public Theatre createTheatre(final String theatreName) {
45	        int theatreId = theatreCounter.incrementAndGet(); // Generate unique ID
46	        Theatre theatre = new Theatre(theatreId, theatreName);
47	        theatres.put(theatreId, theatre); // Store theatre in map
48	        return theatre;
49	    }
50	    // Creates a new screen in the given theatre and links it
51	    public Screen createScreenInTheatre(final String screenName, final Theatre theatre) {
52	        Screen screen = createScreen(screenName, theatre); // Create screen
53	        theatre.addScreen(screen); // Add to theatre
54	        return screen;
55	    }
56	    // Creates a new seat in the given screen and stores it
57	    public Seat createSeatInScreen(final Integer rowNo, SeatCategory seatCategory,  final Screen screen) {
58	        int seatId = seatCounter.incrementAndGet(); // Generate unique seat ID
59	        Seat seat = new Seat(seatId, rowNo, seatCategory);
60	        seats.put(seatId, seat); // Store seat in map
61	        screen.addSeat(seat); // Link seat to screen
62	        return seat;
63	    }
64	    // Private helper to create a screen with unique ID and store it
65	    private Screen createScreen(final String screenName, final Theatre theatre) {
66	        int screenId = screenCounter.incrementAndGet(); // Generate unique ID
67	        Screen screen = new Screen(screenId, screenName, theatre);
68	        screens.put(screenId, screen); // Store screen in map
69	        return screen;
70	    }
71	}
```

‍

_Show Service_

Java

```
1public class ShowService {
2	    private final Map<Integer, Show> shows;  // Map to hold all created shows (key = show ID)
3	    private final AtomicInteger showCounter;  // Counter to generate unique IDs for each show
4	    // Constructor initializing the shows map and show counter
5	    public ShowService() {
6	        this.shows = new HashMap<>();
7	        this.showCounter = new AtomicInteger(0);
8	    }
9	    // Retrieves a show by ID, throws exception if not found
10	    public Show getShow(final int showId) throws Exception {
11	        if (!shows.containsKey(showId)) {
12	            throw new Exception("Show with ID " + showId + " not found.");
13	        }
14	        return shows.get(showId);
15	    }
16	    public Show createShow(final Movie movie, final Screen screen, final Date startTime, final Integer durationInSeconds) {
17	        // Generate a unique show ID
18	        int showId = showCounter.incrementAndGet();
19	        // Create and store the new show
20	        final Show show = new Show(showId, movie, screen, startTime, durationInSeconds);
21	        this.shows.put(showId, show);
22	        return show;
23	    }
24	    private List<Show> getShowsForScreen(final Screen screen) {
25	        final List<Show> response = new ArrayList<>();
26	        for (Show show : shows.values()) {
27	            if (show.getScreen().getScreenId() == screen.getScreenId()) { // Compare by screen ID
28	                response.add(show);
29	            }
30	        }
31	        return response;
32	    }
33	}
```

‍

_Seat Availibilty service_

Java

```
1public class SeatAvailabilityService {
2	    // Dependency for checking booked seats
3	    private final BookingService bookingService;
4	    // Dependency for checking currently locked (but not yet booked) seats
5	    private final ISeatLockProvider seatLockProvider;
6	    // Constructor to initialize dependencies
7	    public SeatAvailabilityService(final BookingService bookingService, final ISeatLockProvider seatLockProvider) {
8	        this.bookingService = bookingService;
9	        this.seatLockProvider = seatLockProvider;
10	    }
11	    public List<Seat> getAvailableSeats(final Show show) {
12	        // Fetch all seats for the show’s screen
13	        final List<Seat> allSeats = show.getScreen().getSeats();
14
15
16	        // Get the list of currently unavailable seats (booked or locked)
17	        final List<Seat> unavailableSeats = getUnavailableSeats(show);
18	        // Start with all seats, and remove unavailable ones
19	        final List<Seat> availableSeats = new ArrayList<>(allSeats);
20	        availableSeats.removeAll(unavailableSeats);
21	        return availableSeats;
22	    }
23
24
25	    private List<Seat> getUnavailableSeats(final Show show) {
26	        // Seats that are already booked
27	        final List<Seat> unavailableSeats = bookingService.getBookedSeats(show);
28
29
30	        // Seats that are locked (e.g., being held in another user’s session)
31	        unavailableSeats.addAll(seatLockProvider.getLockedSeats(show));
32	        return unavailableSeats;
33	    }
34	}
```

‍

_Booking Service_

Java

```
1public class BookingService {
2	    // Stores all bookings made across shows (key = booking ID)
3	    // Changed to a thread-safe concurrent map.
4	    private final Map<String, Booking> showBookings;
5	    // Provider responsible for handling temporary seat locks
6	    private final ISeatLockProvider seatLockProvider;
7	    // Atomic integer to generate unique booking IDs
8	    private final AtomicInteger bookingIdCounter = new AtomicInteger(1);
9	    // Constructor to initialize dependencies
10	    public BookingService(ISeatLockProvider seatLockProvider) {
11	        this.seatLockProvider = seatLockProvider;
12	        this.showBookings = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
13	    }
14
15	    public Booking getBooking(final String bookingId) throws Exception  {
16	        if (!showBookings.containsKey(bookingId)) {
17	            throw new Exception("No Booking exists for the ID : " + bookingId);
18	        }
19	        return showBookings.get(bookingId);
20	    }
21
22	    public List<Booking> getAllBookings(final Show show) {
23	        List<Booking> response = new ArrayList<>();
24	        for (Booking booking : showBookings.values()) {
25	            if (booking.getShow().equals(show)) response.add(booking);
26	        }
27	        return response;
28	    }
29
30	    public Booking createBooking(final User user, final Show show, final List<Seat> seats) throws Exception {
31	        // Check if any requested seat is already booked
32	        if (isAnySeatAlreadyBooked(show, seats)) throw new Exception("Seat Already Booked");
33	        // Lock the seats temporarily for the user (this will throw an exception if any seat is already locked)
34	        seatLockProvider.lockSeats(show, seats, user);
35	        // Create a new booking with a unique booking ID using AtomicInteger
36	        final String bookingId = String.valueOf(bookingIdCounter.getAndIncrement());
37	        final Booking newBooking = new Booking(bookingId, show, user, seats);
38	        // Save the booking
39	        showBookings.put(bookingId, newBooking);
40	        return newBooking;
41	    }
42
43	    public List<Seat> getBookedSeats(final Show show) {
44	        return getAllBookings(show).stream()
45	                .filter(Booking::isConfirmed)      // Only confirmed bookings
46	                .map(Booking::getSeatsBooked)      // Extract booked seats
47	                .flatMap(Collection::stream)       // Flatten seat lists
48	                .collect(Collectors.toList());
49	    }
50
51	    public void confirmBooking(final Booking booking, final User user) throws Exception {
52	        if (!booking.getUser().equals(user)) {
53	            throw new Exception("Cannot confirm a booking made by another user"); // User mismatch
54	        }
55	        // Validate locks for each seat
56	        for (Seat seat : booking.getSeatsBooked()) {
57	            if (!seatLockProvider.validateLock(booking.getShow(), seat, user)) {
58	                throw new Exception("Acquired Lock is either invalid or has Expired");
59	            }
60	        }
61	        // Mark booking as confirmed
62	        booking.confirmBooking();
63	    }
64
65	    private boolean isAnySeatAlreadyBooked(final Show show, final List<Seat> seats) {
66	        final List<Seat> bookedSeats = getBookedSeats(show);
67	        for (Seat seat : seats) {
68	            if (bookedSeats.contains(seat)) return true;
69	        }
70	        return false;
71	    }
72	}
```

‍

**• Payment Service :** 

• _Strategy Pattern for Payments :_

Payment Strategy Interface : 

Java

```
1public interface PaymentStrategy {
2			    boolean processPayment();
3			}
```

‍

**• Concrete Payment Strategies :** 

_Debit Card Strategy :_ 

Java

```
1public class DebitCardStrategy implements PaymentStrategy {
2			    @Override
3			    public boolean processPayment() {
4			        // In a real-world scenario, this would include logic to integrate with a payment gateway,
5			        // validate card details, handle 3D secure authentication, check balance, and process the transaction.
6			        return true;
7			    }
8			}
```

‍

_UPI stratergy:_

Java

```
1public class UpiStrategy implements PaymentStrategy {
2  @Override
3  public boolean processPayment() {
4    // In a real-world scenario, this would include logic to integrate with a
5    // payment gateway, validate card details, handle 3D secure authentication,
6    // check balance, and process the transaction.
7    return false;
8  }
9}
10
11public class PaymentService {
12  // Keeps track of how many times payment has failed for a particular booking.
13  Map<Booking, Integer> bookingFailures;
14
15
16  // The strategy which the user will decide to do the payment
17  private final PaymentStrategy paymentStrategy;
18  private BookingService bookingService;
19
20  public PaymentService(
21      PaymentStrategy paymentStrategy, BookingService bookingService) {
22    this.bookingFailures = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
23    this.paymentStrategy = paymentStrategy;
24    this.bookingService = bookingService;
25  }
26
27  // Called when payment fails for a booking attempt.
28  public void processPaymentFailed(final String bookingId, final User user)
29      throws Exception {
30    // Only the user who initiated the booking is allowed to report failure.
31    Booking booking = bookingService.getBooking(bookingId);
32    if (!booking.getUser().equals(user)) {
33      throw new Exception("Only the booking owner can report payment failure.");
34    }
35    // Initialize failure count for the booking if it's the first failure.
36    if (!bookingFailures.containsKey(booking))
37      bookingFailures.put(booking, 0);
38
39    // Increment failure count.
40    final Integer currentFailuresCount = bookingFailures.get(booking);
41    final Integer newFailuresCount = currentFailuresCount + 1;
42    bookingFailures.put(booking, newFailuresCount);
43    System.out.println(
44        "Could not process the payment for Booking with ID : " + bookingId);
45  }
46
47  public void processPayment(final String bookingId, final User user)
48      throws Exception {
49    if (paymentStrategy.processPayment()) {
50      bookingService.confirmBooking(bookingService.getBooking(bookingId), user);
51    } else {
52      processPaymentFailed(bookingId, user);
53    }
54  }
55}
```

_• Show Controller :_ 

Java

```
1public class ShowController {
2	    // Dependencies injected for handling show operations, theatre data, movie data, and seat availability.
3	    private final SeatAvailabilityService seatAvailabilityService;
4	    private final ShowService showService;
5	    private final TheatreService theatreService;
6	    private final MovieService movieService;
7	    // Constructor to inject all required services
8	    public ShowController(SeatAvailabilityService seatAvailabilityService, ShowService showService,
9	                          TheatreService theatreService, MovieService movieService) {
10	        this.seatAvailabilityService = seatAvailabilityService;
11	        this.showService = showService;
12	        this.theatreService = theatreService;
13	        this.movieService = movieService;
14	    }
15	    public int createShow(final int movieId, final int screenId, final Date startTime,
16	                             final Integer durationInSeconds) throws Exception{
17	        final Screen screen = theatreService.getScreen(screenId);
18	        final Movie movie = movieService.getMovie(movieId);
19	        return showService.createShow(movie, screen, startTime, durationInSeconds).getId();
20	    }
21	    public List<Integer> getAvailableSeats(final int showId) throws Exception{
22	        final Show show = showService.getShow(showId);
23	        final List<Seat> availableSeats = seatAvailabilityService.getAvailableSeats(show);
24	        return availableSeats.stream().map(Seat::getSeatId).collect(Collectors.toList());
25	    }
26	}
```

_Booking Controller_

Java

```
1public class BookingController {
2	    // Services required to handle booking-related operations
3	    private final ShowService showService;
4	    private final BookingService bookingService;
5	    private final TheatreService theatreService;
6	    public BookingController(final ShowService showService, final BookingService bookingService,
7	                             final TheatreService theatreService){
8	        this.showService = showService;
9	        this.bookingService = bookingService;
10	        this.theatreService = theatreService;
11	    }
12	    public String createBooking(final User user, final int showId, final List<Integer> seatsIds) throws Exception{
13	        final Show show = showService.getShow(showId); // Retrieve the show object
14	        // Convert seat IDs to Seat objects
15	        final List<Seat> seats = new ArrayList<>();
16	        for (Integer seatsId : seatsIds) {
17	            Seat seat = theatreService.getSeat(seatsId);
18	            seats.add(seat);
19	        }
20	        return bookingService.createBooking(user, show, seats).getId(); // Create and return booking ID
21	    }
22	}
```
